proposals.title: Auto-release claims on node completion
proposals.source: corruption-detector finding: claimed-completed warning on iter-9-mine
proposals.description: The complete command should auto-release the claim for the completed node. Currently claims persist after completion, triggering claimed-completed warnings from the corruption detector.
proposals.expectedImpact: Eliminates claimed-completed warnings. Reduces stale claim accumulation in token store.
proposals.successCriteria: corruption-detector reports zero claimed-completed warnings after node completion

proposals.title: Consolidate latency improvement with regression guard
proposals.source: iter-9-mine metrics: P50 dropped from 671ms (iter-8) to 134ms (iter-9)
proposals.description: 5x latency improvement observed but no regression guard exists. Add a latency threshold assertion to the integration test suite so future changes cannot silently regress P50 above 300ms.
proposals.expectedImpact: Prevents latency regression. Documents performance baseline.
proposals.successCriteria: Integration test fails if any CLI command P50 exceeds 300ms

proposals.title: Wrap chart output in CLI envelope
proposals.source: envelope-validation: chart classified as text-exempt, mining classified as mixed
proposals.description: Chart currently emits render text to stdout and appends JSON envelope. Wrapping chart render inside the envelope's render field (already supported by CliEnvelope.render) would make all commands consistently envelope-wrapped. Chart is currently exempt from compliance checks.
proposals.expectedImpact: 100% envelope coverage with zero exemptions. Consistent parsing for all CLI consumers.
proposals.successCriteria: chart output parses as valid CliEnvelope with render.body containing the chart text

rationale: Iter-9 brought CLI hardening modules online (atomic claims, corruption detection, envelope validation, metaflow instrumentation). The audit is clean. Proposals focus on tightening the new infrastructure: auto-releasing claims (P9-001) prevents the one warning class found, regression guarding the latency win (P9-002) protects the 5x improvement, and envelope-wrapping chart (P9-003) closes the last compliance gap.
